blocks.text: The executable SSOT for provider native config file plans is `internal/workdir/native_config_plan.riido.json` (`riido-native-config-plan.v1`). `go generate ./internal/workdir` compiles it through `tools/riidogen/templates/native_config_plan.go.gotmpl` into `internal/workdir/native_config_plan_gen.go`.

`riido-native-config-manifest.v1` is the current evidence for provider-native config materialization. The same manifest content is written under `workdir/.riido/native-config-manifest.json` and `native-config/.riido/native-config-manifest.json`.

rows: Provider family such as `claude`, `codex`, `openclaw`, `cursor`, or fallback.
Protocol kind selected by C3; omitted when empty.
Provider auto-loaded instruction file such as `CLAUDE.md`, `AGENTS.md`, or `GEMINI.md`.
`instruction-only` or a provider-native hook materialization mode such as `claude-command-hooks`.
Provider config home relative path when task-scoped config home is explicitly materialized.
Provider settings/config files that the provider can read.
Provider-native hook script files referenced by settings.
Where the SaaS source placed the telemetry contract.
Runtime config workflow branch; defaults to `default`.
Deterministically materialized relative file paths, including the manifest itself.
Claude command hook files may materialize only when C7 allows the `claude:command-hooks:audit` surface; otherwise hook mode falls back to `instruction-only`.
Codex task-scoped `.codex/config.toml` or `CODEX_HOME` overlay is not materialized by C6; C4 owns the full-access runtime envelope.
Current default is provider-neutral instruction file injection only.
